text.title: rust-tokio-async Failure Modes
text.summary: Failure modes observed when applying the rust-tokio-async methodology. Each pairs symptom + root-cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Service calls try_join! over 10K items; runtime allocates 10K tasks and OOMs the pod.
antipattern.fix: Switch to stream::iter(...).buffer_unordered(N) with N tied to backend pool size.

antipattern.symptom: Argon2 hashing inside an async handler stalls all other requests on the same worker.
External HTTP call hangs forever; the worker pool exhausts.
let g = mutex.lock(); some_io.await; deadlocks on multi_thread runtime.
antipattern.fix: Narrow the critical section to before .await, or use tokio::sync::Mutex.

antipattern.symptom: select! between an HTTP read and shutdown drops the read mid-buffer.
antipattern.fix: Document the unsafe branch + structure as a loop with biased select, OR move the read into a JoinSet.
